subject: Mathematics
q: The number of solutions of the equation x+2tanx=π2 in the interval 0, 2π is
question: The number of solutions of the equation x+2tanx=π2 in the interval 0, 2π is
solution:
We have x+2tanx=π2

⇒2tanx=π2-x

⇒tanx=-12x+π4

The graph of the two functions is given as

It is clear from the graph that the number of solutions of the given equation is 3.

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mi>cos</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>sin</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> has exactly <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math> solutions in the interval <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="⌉" open="["><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></math>, then the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>n</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mo>(</mo><mi>n</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> belong to :
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mi>cos</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>sin</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> has exactly <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math> solutions in the interval <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="⌉" open="["><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></math>, then the roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>n</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mo>(</mo><mi>n</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> belong to :
solution:
Given: 2sin3x+sin2xcosx+4sinx-4=0

⇒2sin3x+2sinx·cos2x+4sinx-4=0

⇒2sin3x+2sinx·1-sin2x+4sinx-4=0

⇒2sin3x+2sinx-2sin3x+4sinx-4=0

⇒6sinx-4=0

⇒sinx=23

Now, for exactly three solution we get,

⇒n=5 (in the given interval)

So, x2+nx+n-3=0

⇒x2+5x+2=0

⇒x=-5±172

So, the required interval is (-∞,0).

q: The number of elements in the set <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>θ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>π</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><mn>3</mn><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>10</mn><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is ______.
question: The number of elements in the set <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>θ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>π</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><mn>3</mn><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>10</mn><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> is ______.
solution:
3cos22θ+6cos2θ-10cos2θ+5=0

3cos22θ+6cos2θ-51+cos2θ+5=0

cos2θ3cos2θ+1=0

cos2θ=0 or cos2θ=-13

Given θ∈-4π,4π⇒2θ∈-8π,8π

So for 2θ=2n+1π2

 θ=±π4,±3π4,………±15π4 gives 16 solution

Similarly cos2θ=-13 gives 16 solution

hence, total elements in set S will be 32

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo><</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>y</mi><mo><</mo><mi>π</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cos</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>3</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>sin</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mi>y</mi></math> is equal to:
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo><</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>y</mi><mo><</mo><mi>π</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cos</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>3</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>sin</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mi>y</mi></math> is equal to:
solution:
cosx+cosy-cosx+y=32

2cosx+y2·cosx-y2-2cos2x+y2-1=32

2cosx+y2·cosx-y2-2cos2x+y2+1=32

2cosx+y2·cosx-y2-2cos2x+y2=12

4cosx+y2·cosx-y2-4cos2x+y2=1

⇒4cosx+y2·cosx-y2-4cos2x+y2=1=sin2x-y2+cos2x-y2

⇒4cos2x+y2+cos2x-y2-4cosx+y2·cosx-y2+sin2x-y2=0

⇒2cosx+y2-cosx-y22+sin2x-y2=0

⇒sinx-y2=0 and cosx+y2=12cosx-y2

⇒x=y and cosx=12=cosy

∴ sinx=32

⇒sinx+cosy=1+32

option 4

q: The number of solutions of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced open="[" close="]" separators="|"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>5</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>5</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math> is:
question: The number of solutions of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced open="[" close="]" separators="|"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>5</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>5</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math> is:
solution:
The given trigonometric equation can be written as
1-cos23x+sin4x=0

⇒ sin23x+sin4x=0

⇒sin3x=0 and sinx=0 , x∈-5π2,5π2 

In given interval sinx=0 at x=-2π, -π, 0, π, 2π and these values are also satisfied by sin3x=0.
Hence total number of solutions is 5.

subject: Mathematics
q: If $2 \cos \theta+\sin \theta=1\left(\theta \neq \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$, then $7 \cos \theta+6 \sin \theta$ is equal to:
question: If $2 \cos \theta+\sin \theta=1\left(\theta \neq \frac{\pi}{2}\right)$, then $7 \cos \theta+6 \sin \theta$ is equal to:
solution:
Given $2 \cos \theta+\sin \theta=1$
Squaring both sides, we get
$$
\begin{aligned}
&(2 \cos \theta+\sin \theta)^2=1^2 \\
&\Rightarrow 4 \cos ^2 \theta+\sin ^2 \theta+4 \sin \theta \cos \theta=1 \\
&\Rightarrow 3 \cos ^2 \theta+\left(\cos ^2 \theta+\sin ^2 \theta\right)+4 \sin \theta \cos \theta \\
&=1 \\
&\Rightarrow 3 \cos ^2 \theta+\not \lambda+4 \sin \theta \cos \theta=1 \\
&\Rightarrow 3 \cos ^2 \theta+4 \sin \theta \cos \theta=0 \\
&\Rightarrow \cos \theta(3 \cos \theta+4 \sin \theta)=0 \\
&\Rightarrow 3 \cos \theta+4 \sin \theta=0 \\
&\Rightarrow 3 \cos \theta=-4 \sin \theta \\
&\Rightarrow \frac{-3}{4}=\tan \theta=\sqrt{\sec ^2 \theta-1}=\frac{-3}{4} \\
&\left(\because \tan ^2 \theta=\sqrt{\sec ^2 \theta-1}\right) \\
&\Rightarrow \sec ^2 \theta-1=\left(\frac{-3}{4}\right)^2=\frac{9}{16} \\
&\Rightarrow \sec 2 \theta=\frac{9}{16}+1=\frac{25}{16} \Rightarrow \sec \theta=\frac{5}{4}
\end{aligned}
$$
or $\cos \theta=\frac{4}{5}$
Now, $\sin ^2 \theta+\cos ^2 \theta=1$
$$
\begin{aligned}
&\Rightarrow \sin ^2 \theta+\left(\frac{4}{5}\right)^2=1 \\
&\sin ^2 \theta+\frac{4}{5}=1 \Rightarrow \sin ^2 \theta=1-\frac{16}{25}=\frac{9}{25} \\
&\sin \theta=\pm \frac{3}{5}
\end{aligned}
$$
Taking $\quad\left(\sin \theta=+\frac{3}{5}\right) \quad$ because $\left(\sin \theta=-\frac{3}{5}\right)$ cannot satisfy the given equation.
Therefore; $7 \cos \theta+6 \sin \theta$
$$
=7 \times \frac{4}{5}+6 \times \frac{3}{5}=\frac{28}{5}+\frac{18}{5}=\frac{46}{5}
$$

subject: Mathematics
q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="["><mrow><mo>-</mo><mi>π</mi><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>4</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math>. Then the number of elements in the set <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mi>θ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mo>:</mo><mi>tan</mi><mi>θ</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mn>5</mn></msqrt><mi>tan</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msqrt><mn>5</mn></msqrt><mo>-</mo><mi>tan</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math> is _____ .
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="["><mrow><mo>-</mo><mi>π</mi><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>4</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math>. Then the number of elements in the set <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mi>θ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mo>:</mo><mi>tan</mi><mi>θ</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mn>5</mn></msqrt><mi>tan</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msqrt><mn>5</mn></msqrt><mo>-</mo><mi>tan</mi><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math> is _____ .
solution:
Given,

 S=-π,π2--π2,-π4,-3π4,π4 and the set A=θ∈S:tanθ1+5tan2θ=5-tan2θ

Now let tanα=5

So, tanθ1+5tan2θ=5-tan2θ becomes,

⇒tanθ1+tanαtan2θ=tanα-tan2θ

On rearranging we get,

⇒tanθ=tanα-tan2θ1+tanαtan2θ

On using formula tanA-B=tanA-tanB1-tanAtanB we get,

⇒tanθ=tanα-2θ

⇒α-2θ=nπ+θ

⇒  3θ=α-nπ

⇒  θ=α3-nπ3; n∈Z

If θ∈[-π,π/2) then n=0,1,2,3,4 are acceptable solution

So, total 5 solutions are possible.

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $S=\left\{\sin ^2 2 \theta:\left(\sin ^4 \theta+\cos ^4 \theta\right) x^2+(\sin 2 \theta) x+\left(\sin ^6 \theta+\cos ^6 \theta\right)=0\right.$ has real roots $\}$. If $\alpha$ and $\beta$ be the smallest and largest elements of the set $S$, respectively, then $3\left((\alpha-2)^2+(\beta-1)^2\right)$ equals _________
question: Let $S=\left\{\sin ^2 2 \theta:\left(\sin ^4 \theta+\cos ^4 \theta\right) x^2+(\sin 2 \theta) x+\left(\sin ^6 \theta+\cos ^6 \theta\right)=0\right.$ has real roots $\}$. If $\alpha$ and $\beta$ be the smallest and largest elements of the set $S$, respectively, then $3\left((\alpha-2)^2+(\beta-1)^2\right)$ equals _________
solution: $\begin{aligned} & D=(\sin 2 \theta)^2-4\left(1-\frac{\sin ^2 2 \theta}{2}\right)\left(1-\frac{3}{4} \sin ^2 2 \theta\right) \\ & =(\sin 2 \theta)^2-4\left(1-\frac{5}{4} \sin ^2 2 \theta+\frac{3}{8} \sin ^4 2 \theta\right) \\ & D=-\frac{3}{2} \sin ^4 2 \theta+6 \sin ^2 2 \theta-4>0 \\ & 3 \sin ^4 2 \theta-12 \sin ^2 2 \theta+8 < 0 \\ & \sin ^2 2 \theta=\frac{12 \pm \sqrt{12^2-12.8}}{6}=\frac{12 \pm 4 \sqrt{3}}{6}=\frac{6 \pm 2 \sqrt{3}}{3} \\ & \sin ^2 2 \theta=2 \pm \frac{2}{\sqrt{3}}, \text { but } \sin ^2 2 \theta \in[0,1] \\ & \therefore \alpha=2-\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}}, \beta=1 \rightarrow(\alpha-2)^2=\frac{4}{3},(\beta-1)^2=0 \\ & 3(\alpha-2)^2+(\beta-1)^2=4\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: If $0 < x < \pi$ and $\cos x+\sin x=\frac{1}{2}$, then $\tan x$ is
question: If $0 < x < \pi$ and $\cos x+\sin x=\frac{1}{2}$, then $\tan x$ is
solution:
$\cos x+\sin x=\frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow 1+\sin 2 x=\frac{1}{4} \Rightarrow \sin 2 x=-\frac{3}{4}$, so $x$ is obtuse and $\frac{2 \tan x}{1+\tan ^2 x}=-\frac{3}{4} \Rightarrow 3 \tan ^2 x+8 \tan x+3=0$
$\therefore \tan x=\frac{-8 \pm \sqrt{64-36}}{6}=\frac{-4 \pm \sqrt{7}}{3}$
$\because \tan x < 0 \quad \therefore \tan x=\frac{-4-\sqrt{7}}{3}$

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo><</mo><mi>α</mi><mo><</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math> is the solution of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mi>cos</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mi>sin</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>tan</mi><mi>α</mi></math> is
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo><</mo><mi>α</mi><mo><</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math> is the solution of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mi>cos</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>5</mn><mi>sin</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math>, then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>tan</mi><mi>α</mi></math> is
solution:
Given: 4cosθ+5sinθ=1

⇒41-tan2θ21+tan2θ2+52tanθ21+tan2θ2=1

⇒5tan2θ2-10tanθ2-3=0

⇒tanθ2=10±100-45-32×5

⇒tanθ2=10±16010

⇒tanθ2=5±405

It is given that, α∈-π2,π2

⇒α2∈-π4,π4

⇒tanα2∈-1,1

⇒tanα2=5-405

We know that, tanθ=2tanθ21-tan2θ2

⇒tanα=25-4051-5-4052

⇒tanα=25-4052405-85

⇒tanα=5-4040-4

⇒tanα=40-2024

⇒tanα=10-1012

subject: Mathematics
q: The number of elements in the set $\left\{x \in\left[0,180^{\circ}\right]: \tan \left(x+100^{\circ}\right)=\tan \left(x+50^{\circ}\right) \tan x \tan \left(x-50^{\circ}\right)\right\}$ is $\_\_\_\_$.
question: The number of elements in the set $\left\{x \in\left[0,180^{\circ}\right]: \tan \left(x+100^{\circ}\right)=\tan \left(x+50^{\circ}\right) \tan x \tan \left(x-50^{\circ}\right)\right\}$ is $\_\_\_\_$.
solution:
$\frac{\tan(x+100°)}{\tan x} = \tan(x+50°)\tan(x-50°)$
$\frac{\sin(x+100°)\cos x}{\cos(x+100°)\sin x} = \frac{\sin(x+50°)\sin(x-50°)}{\cos(x+50°)\cos(x-50°)}$
Applying Componendo & Dividendo:
$\frac{\sin(2x+100°)}{\sin 100°} = \frac{\cos 100°}{-\cos 2x}$
$2\sin(2x+100°)\cos 2x + \sin 200° = 0$
$\sin(4x+100°) + \sin 100° + \sin 200° = 0$
$\sin(4x+100°) = -2\sin 150° \cos 50°$
$\sin(4x+100°) = -\cos 50° = \sin(-40°)$
$\therefore 4x + 100° = n\pi + (-1)^n(-40°)$
$x = \frac{n\pi + (-1)^{n+1}(40°) - 100°}{4}$
$\therefore x = 30°, 55°, 120°, 145°$ in $(0, \pi)$
$\therefore$ Number of solutions $= 4$

subject: Mathematics
q: The number of solutions of the equation, $\sin ^{-1} x=2 \tan ^{-1} x$ (in principal values) is :
question: The number of solutions of the equation, $\sin ^{-1} x=2 \tan ^{-1} x$ (in principal values) is :
solution:
Given equation is
$$
\sin ^{-1} x=2 \tan ^{-1} x
$$
Now, this equation has only one solution.
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \therefore \quad \text { LHS }=\sin ^{-1} 1=\frac{\pi}{2} \\
& \text { and } \text { RHS }=2 \tan ^{-1} 1=2 \times \frac{\pi}{4}=\frac{\pi}{2}
\end{aligned}
$$
Also, $x=1$ gives angle value as $\frac{\pi}{4}$ and $\frac{5 \pi}{4}$ $\frac{5 \pi}{4}$ is outside the principal value.

subject: Mathematics
q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced><mrow><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>:</mo><msup><mn>9</mn><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>tan</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mn>9</mn><mrow><msup><mi>tan</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><munder><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>S</mi></mrow></munder><msup><mi>tan</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>6</mn></mfrac><mo>(</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>14</mn><msup><mo>)</mo><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced><mrow><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>:</mo><msup><mn>9</mn><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>tan</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mn>9</mn><mrow><msup><mi>tan</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><munder><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>S</mi></mrow></munder><msup><mi>tan</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mi>x</mi><mn>3</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>6</mn></mfrac><mo>(</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>14</mn><msup><mo>)</mo><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to
solution:
Given,

91-tan2x+9tan2x=10

Now let 9tan2x=t, then above equation will be,

9t+t=10

⇒t2-10t+9=0

⇒t=9 or t=1

So, when 9tan2x=9⇒tan2x=1

⇒tanx=±1⇒x=±π4, as given x∈-π2,π2

Now when 9tan2x=1

⇒tan2x=0⇒x=0

Hence, β=∑x∈Sx3=tan203+tan2π12+tan2-π12

=0+22-32

=14-83

So, the value of 16β-142=64×36=32

Hence this is the correct option.

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $\mathrm{A}=\{\theta: \sin (\theta)=\tan (\theta)\}$ and $\mathrm{B}=(\theta: \cos (\theta)=$ 1\} be two sets. Then:
question: Let $\mathrm{A}=\{\theta: \sin (\theta)=\tan (\theta)\}$ and $\mathrm{B}=(\theta: \cos (\theta)=$ 1\} be two sets. Then:
solution:
Let $\mathrm{A}=\{\theta: \sin \theta=\tan \theta\}$ and $\mathrm{B}=\{\theta: \cos \theta=1\}$
Now, $A=\left\{\theta: \sin \theta=\frac{\sin \theta}{\cos \theta}\right\}$ $=\{\theta: \sin \theta(\cos \theta-1)=0\}$ $=\{\theta=0, \pi, 2 \pi, 3 \pi, \ldots \ldots\}$
For $\mathrm{B}: \cos \theta=1 \Rightarrow \theta=\pi, 2 \pi, 4 \pi, \ldots \ldots$
This shows that $\mathrm{A}$ is not contained in B. i.e. $\mathrm{A} \not \subset \mathrm{B}$. but $\mathrm{B} \subset \mathrm{A}$.

q: The number of elements in the set <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mi>θ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mo>[</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi><mo>]</mo><mo>:</mo><mn>3</mn><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>4</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>6</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is

solution:
Given,

3cos4θ-5cos2θ-2sin6θ+2=0

⇒cos2θ3cos2θ-5-2sin6θ+2=0

⇒1-sin2θ3-3sin2θ-5-2sin6θ+2=0

⇒sin2θ-13sin2θ+2-2sin6θ+2=0

Let sin2θ=t
⇒(t-1)(3t+2)-2t3+2=0

⇒(t-1)(3t+2)-2t3-1=0

We know that a3-b3=a-ba2+ab+b2

⇒(t-1)(3t+2)-2t-1t2+t+1=0

⇒(t-1)3t+2-2t2+t+1=0

(t-1)2t2-t=0

⇒t=0,1,12

For sin2θ=0, θ=0,π,2π

sin2θ=1, θ=π2,3π2

sin2θ=12, θ=π6,5π6,7π6,11π6

∴ Total solution =9

Hence this is the required option.

q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi> </mi><mi>S</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mi>θ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>:</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>sin</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>.</mo></math> Then the sum of the elements of <math><mi>S</mi></math> is:
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi> </mi><mi>S</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mi>θ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced close="]" open="[" separators="|"><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>:</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>sin</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>.</mo></math> Then the sum of the elements of <math><mi>S</mi></math> is:
solution:
The given equation 2cos2θ+3sinθ=0 can be written as

21-sin2θ+3sinθ=0

⇒2sin2θ-3sinθ-2=0

⇒2sinθ+1sinθ-2=0

⇒sinθ=2, -12, sinθ≠2

⇒sinθ=-12

Hence, solutions in -2π, 2π are
-π+π6, -π6, π+π6, 2π-π6

Sum of the elements is =2π.

q: The number of values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> in the interval <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>7</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>4</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math> for which <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>14</mn><msup><mo>cosec</mo><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>21</mn><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></math> holds, is ______.
question: The number of values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> in the interval <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>7</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>4</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math> for which <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>14</mn><msup><mo>cosec</mo><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>21</mn><mo>-</mo><mn>4</mn><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></math> holds, is ______.
solution:
Given x∈π4,7π4

14cosec2x-2sin2x=21-4cos2x

=21-41-sin2x

=17+4sin2x

14sin2x-6sin2x=17

Let sin2x=p

14p-6p=17⇒14-6p2=17p

6p2+17p-14=0

p=-72,23⇒sin2x=23

⇒sinx=±23

∴  There are 4 solutions

q:
Consider the following two statements.
Statement $\boldsymbol{p}$ :
The value of $\sin 120^{\circ}$ can be divided by taking $\theta=240^{\circ}$ in the equation
$$
2 \sin \frac{\theta}{2}=\sqrt{1+\sin \theta}-\sqrt{1-\sin \theta} .
$$
Statement $\boldsymbol{q}$ :
The angles $A, B, C$ and $D$ of any quadrilateral $A B C D$ satisfy the equation
$$
\cos \left(\frac{1}{2}(A+C)\right)+\cos \left(\frac{1}{2}(B+D)\right)=0
$$
Then the truth values of $p$ and $q$ are respectively.
question:
Consider the following two statements.
Statement $\boldsymbol{p}$ :
The value of $\sin 120^{\circ}$ can be divided by taking $\theta=240^{\circ}$ in the equation
$$
2 \sin \frac{\theta}{2}=\sqrt{1+\sin \theta}-\sqrt{1-\sin \theta} .
$$
Statement $\boldsymbol{q}$ :
The angles $A, B, C$ and $D$ of any quadrilateral $A B C D$ satisfy the equation
$$
\cos \left(\frac{1}{2}(A+C)\right)+\cos \left(\frac{1}{2}(B+D)\right)=0
$$
Then the truth values of $p$ and $q$ are respectively.
solution:
Statement $p$ :
$$
\sin 120^{\circ}=\cos 30^{\circ}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \Rightarrow 2 \sin 120^{\circ}=\sqrt{3}
$$
So, $\sqrt{1+\sin 240^{\circ}}-\sqrt{1-\sin 240^{\circ}}=$
$$
\sqrt{\frac{1-\sqrt{3}}{2}}-\sqrt{\frac{1+\sqrt{3}}{2}} \neq \sqrt{3}
$$
Statement $\mathbf{q}$ :
So, $A+B+C+D=2 \pi$
$$
\begin{aligned}
&\Rightarrow \frac{A+C}{2}+\frac{B+D}{2}=\pi \\
&\Rightarrow \cos \left(\frac{A+C}{2}\right)+\cos \left(\frac{B+D}{2}\right) \\
&=\cos \left(\frac{A+C}{2}\right)-\cos \left(\frac{A+C}{2}\right)=0
\end{aligned}
$$
Therefore, statement $p$ is false and statement $q$ is true.

subject: Mathematics
q:
Statement-1: The number of common solutions of the trigonometric equations $2 \sin ^2 \theta$ $-\cos 2 \theta=0$ and $2 \cos ^2 \theta-3 \sin \theta=0$ in the interval $[0,2 \pi]$ is two.
Statement-2: The number of solutions of the equation, $2 \cos ^2 \theta-3 \sin \theta=0$ in the interval $[0, \pi]$ is two.
question:
Statement-1: The number of common solutions of the trigonometric equations $2 \sin ^2 \theta$ $-\cos 2 \theta=0$ and $2 \cos ^2 \theta-3 \sin \theta=0$ in the interval $[0,2 \pi]$ is two.
Statement-2: The number of solutions of the equation, $2 \cos ^2 \theta-3 \sin \theta=0$ in the interval $[0, \pi]$ is two.
options: Statement-1 is true; Statement-2 is true; Statement-2 is a correct explanation for statement-1.
Statement-1 is true; Statement-2 is true; Statement-2 is not a correct explanation for statement-1.
Statement-1 is false; Statement-2 is true.
Statement-1 is true; Statement-2 is false.
solution:
$2 \sin ^2 \theta-\cos 2 \theta=0$
$\Rightarrow 2 \sin ^2 \theta-\left(1-2 \sin ^2 \theta\right)=0$
$\Rightarrow 2 \sin ^2 \theta-1+2 \sin ^2 \theta=0$
$\Rightarrow 4 \sin ^2 \theta=1 \Rightarrow \sin \theta=\pm \frac{1}{2}$
$\therefore \theta=\frac{\pi}{4}, \frac{3 \pi}{4}, \frac{5 \pi}{4}, \frac{7 \pi}{4}, \theta \in[0,2 \pi]$
$\therefore \theta=\frac{\pi}{6}, \frac{5 \pi}{6}, \frac{7 \pi}{6}, \frac{11 \pi}{6}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text { Now } 2 \cos ^2 \theta-3 \sin \theta=0 \\
\Rightarrow & 2\left(1-\sin ^2 \theta\right)-3 \sin \theta=0 \\
\Rightarrow & -2 \sin ^2 \theta-3 \sin \theta+2=0 \\
\Rightarrow & -2 \sin ^2 \theta-4 \sin \theta+\sin \theta+2=0 \\
\Rightarrow & 2 \sin ^2 \theta-\sin \theta+4 \sin \theta-2=0 \\
\Rightarrow & \sin \theta(2 \sin \theta-1)+2(2 \sin \theta-1)=0 \\
\Rightarrow & \sin \theta=\frac{1}{2},-2
\end{aligned}
$$
But $\sin \theta=-2$, is not possible
$$
\therefore \quad \sin \theta=\frac{1}{2}, \Rightarrow \theta=\frac{\pi}{6}, \frac{5 \pi}{6}
$$
Hence, there are two common solution, there each of the statement-1 and 2 are true but statement- 2 is not a correct explanation for statement-1.

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>tan</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn><mi>sec</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> has exactly <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn></math> solutions in the interval <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math>, for the least value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></munderover><mfrac><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><msup><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi></msup></mfrac></math> is equal to :
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>tan</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>θ</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn><mi>sec</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> has exactly <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn></math> solutions in the interval <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math>, for the least value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi></munderover><mfrac><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><msup><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi></msup></mfrac></math> is equal to :
solution:
Given,

2tan2θ-5secθ-1=0

⇒2sec2θ-1-5secθ-1=0

⇒2sec2θ-5secθ-3=0

⇒2sec2θ-6secθ+secθ-3=0

⇒2secθsecθ-3+1secθ-3=0

⇒(2secθ+1)(secθ-3)=0

⇒secθ=-12,3

⇒cosθ=-2, 13

We know that, -1≤cosθ≤1

⇒cosθ=13

For 7 solutions n=13

So, ∑k=113k2k=S (say)

⇒S=12+222+323+….+13213

⇒12 S=122+123+…..+12213+13214

⇒S-S2=12+222+323+….+13213-122+123+…..+12213+13214

⇒S2=12+122+323+...+13213-13214

⇒S2=12·1-12131-12-13214

⇒S2=12·213-121312-13214

⇒S=2·213-1213-13213

⇒S=214-2-13213

⇒S=214-15213

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>≤</mo><mi>x</mi><mo><</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math> then the number of values of <math><mi>x</mi></math> for which <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>sin</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>sin</mi><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> is:

solution:
sinx-sin2x+sin3x=0

⇒sinx+sin3x-sin2x=0

⇒2sin2 x .cosx-sin2x=0

⇒sin2x=0 or cosx=12

⇒2x=0 or x=π3

⇒x=0 or  x=π3

∴ Number of solutions =2

q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mi>θ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mo>[</mo><mo>-</mo><mi>π</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>π</mi><mo>]</mo><mo>-</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mo>±</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>:</mo><mi>sin</mi><mi>θ</mi><mi>tan</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>tan</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>T</mi><mo>=</mo><munder><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>θ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>S</mi></mrow></munder><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>T</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>n</mi><mfenced><mi>S</mi></mfenced></math> is equal to
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mi>θ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mo>[</mo><mo>-</mo><mi>π</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>π</mi><mo>]</mo><mo>-</mo><mfenced close="}" open="{"><mrow><mo>±</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>:</mo><mi>sin</mi><mi>θ</mi><mi>tan</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>tan</mi><mi>θ</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>T</mi><mo>=</mo><munder><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>θ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>S</mi></mrow></munder><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>T</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>n</mi><mfenced><mi>S</mi></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
Given sin θ tan θ+tan θ=sin 2θ; θ≠±π2

⇒sin2 θcos θ+sin θcos θ=2 sin θ cos θ

⇒sin θ=0 or sin θcos θ+1cos θ=2 cos θ

⇒θ=0, π, -π or sin θ+1=21-sin2 θ

⇒θ=0, π, -π or 2 sin2 θ +sin θ-1=0

⇒θ=0, π, -π or sin θ=12, sin θ=-1

⇒θ=0, π, -π or θ=π6, 5π6 or θ=-π2 (rejected)

Hence, θ=0, π, -π, π6, 5π6

⇒nS=5

Now, T=Σcos 2θ

=cos 0+cos 2π+cos-2π+cosπ3+cos5π3

=1+1+1+12+12=4

So, T+nS=9

the number of intersection as 8

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cos</mi><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>3</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mi> </mi><mtext>and</mtext><mi> </mi><mi>sin</mi><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>sin</mi><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mi> </mi></math>and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>θ</mi></math> is the arithmetic mean of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mi> </mi><mo>&</mo><mo> </mo><mi>β</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi></math> is equal to:
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cos</mi><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>3</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mi> </mi><mtext>and</mtext><mi> </mi><mi>sin</mi><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>sin</mi><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac><mi> </mi></math>and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>θ</mi></math> is the arithmetic mean of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mi> </mi><mo>&</mo><mo> </mo><mi>β</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>θ</mi></math> is equal to:
solution:
Given 
cos α + cos β = 32
sin α + sin β = 12
⇒2cos(α+β2)cos(α-β2)=32 ......(i) and

2sin⁡(α+β2)cos⁡(α-β2)=12 ....(ii)

Dividing (ii) and (i)

tan α + β2
∴  tan⁡θ =13

sin2θ+cos⁡2θ = 2tan⁡θ1+tan2⁡θ+ 1-tan2⁡θ1+tan2⁡θ=2131+19+1-191+19

= 23+89109=1410=75

subject: Mathematics
q: The number of solutions of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>−</mo><mn>4</mn><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>9</mn><mo>−</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>cos</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>;</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mo>−</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is:
question: The number of solutions of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>−</mo><mn>4</mn><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>9</mn><mo>−</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>cos</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>;</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mo>−</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is:
solution:
Given: 4sin2x-4cos3x+9-4cosx=0

⇒4-4cos2x-4cos3x+9-4cosx=0

⇒4cos3x+4cos2x+4cosx-13=0

⇒4cos3x+4cos2x+4cosx=13

We know that, -1≤cosx≤1

So, the maximum value of LHS is 4+4+4=12

But, RHS is 13.

So, no solutions are possible for the given equation.

subject: Mathematics
q: Number of solutions of $\sqrt{3} \cos 2 \theta+8 \cos \theta+3 \sqrt{3}=0, \theta \in[-3 \pi, 2 \pi]$ is:
question: Number of solutions of $\sqrt{3} \cos 2 \theta+8 \cos \theta+3 \sqrt{3}=0, \theta \in[-3 \pi, 2 \pi]$ is:
solution:
Using $\cos 2\theta = 2\cos^2\theta - 1$:
$2\sqrt{3}\cos^2\theta + 8\cos\theta + 2\sqrt{3} = 0 \Rightarrow \sqrt{3}\cos^2\theta + 4\cos\theta + \sqrt{3} = 0$.
$\cos\theta = \frac{-4 \pm 2}{2\sqrt{3}}$, giving $\cos\theta = -\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}$ or $\cos\theta = -\sqrt{3}$ (rejected).
Let $\alpha = \cos^{-1}(-1/\sqrt{3}) \approx 2.186$ rad. General solution: $\theta = 2n\pi \pm \alpha$.
In $[-3\pi, 2\pi]$: $\theta = \alpha, -\alpha, 2\pi-\alpha, -2\pi+\alpha, -2\pi-\alpha$.
Total $= 5$ solutions.

subject: Mathematics
q: The number of roots of the equation, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mn>81</mn></mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mfenced><mn>81</mn></mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>30</mn></math> in the interval <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to :
question: The number of roots of the equation, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mn>81</mn></mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>sin</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mfenced><mn>81</mn></mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>30</mn></math> in the interval <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to :
solution:
81sin2x+81cos2x=30

81sin2x+81181sin2x=30

81sin2x=t

t+81t=30

t2-30t+81=0

t-3t-27=0

81sin2x=31 or  81sin2x=33

34sin2x=31  or   34sin2x=33

sin2x=14  or  sin2x=34

  

Hence, total 4 solution.
